calendar.matters:
Petition For Distribution In Order To Satisfy Judgment By Transferring Real And Tangible Personal Property To Deanna Ireland And Petition To Distribute The Orcas Island Properties To Beneficiaries On A Pro-Rata Basis
Off Calendar Jul-03-2018 Granted Per Written Order

Notice Of Petition For Distribution Of Trust Funds In Payment Of Judgment And Child Support By Creditor Deanna Ireland
Off Calendar Jul-03-2018 Denied

Petition For Distribution In Order To Satisfy Judgment By Transferring Real And Tangible Personal Property To Deanna Ireland And Petition To Distribute The Orcas Island Properties To Beneficiaries On A Pro-Rata Basis
Off Calendar May-02-2018 Continued To May-07-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Avid Alavi for Scott Hodges.
Daniel Conrad Litigation Counsel for Scott Hodges (Trustee).
Teresa Trucchi appeared by telephone for Deanna Ireland (Petitioner/Creditor).

Mr. Conrad stated that the parties agreed at the last hearing to $35,000.00 for the property and $34,845.00 for the art collection, and together with the $20,000.00 that was distributed in cash, it satisfies the amount in full. Ms. Trucchi stated that they are opposed to anything being released to Ian until the child support judgment from Riverside is satisfied. Ms. Alavi stated that this satisfies one order, that the Trust needs to be distributed, that there are no funds to pay for administration and that Ms. Trucchi can pursue the judgment by going after Ian. Ms. Trucchi stated that they do not oppose distribution, that they oppose any distribution to Ian, that this is why they filed their lien and lodged the judgment, made reference Family Code 4560(b) and stated that child support takes priority. Ms. Trucchi also made reference to Pratt v Ferguson 3Cal.App.5th.102, stated that CCP 709.010 is the authority and that she briefed it in her notice of petition filed on August 9, 2017. Mr. Conrad stated that what's supposed to be distributed is undivided fractional interests in a partial interest in several pieces of property in Washington that are illiquid, that Deanna can then pursue Ian and that there is only one order that is being satisfied. Ms. Trucchi is to provide a proposed order. The Petition for Distribution in Order to Satisfy Judgment, etc., and Petition for Distribution of Trust Funds in Payment of Judgment and Child Support are continued to May 7, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and will be taken under submission on that date.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Notice Of Petition For Distribution Of Trust Funds In Payment Of Judgment And Child Support By Creditor Deanna Ireland
Off Calendar May-02-2018 Continued To May-07-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Petition For Distribution Of Trust Funds In Payment Of Judgment And Child Support By Creditor Deanna Ireland
Off Calendar Mar-05-2018 Continued To Apr-17-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Petition For Distribution Of Trust Funds In Payment Of Judgment And Child Support By Creditor Deanna Ireland
Off Calendar Jan-29-2018 Continued To Mar-05-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Daniel Conrad for Scott Hodges (Successor Trustee).
Teresa Trucchi appeared by telephone for Deanna Ireland (Creditor).

Mr. Conrad stated that they have agreed on distribution values for the lot in Idaho at $35,000.00 and the art collection at $34,845.28, that $20,000.00 has already been distributed, that the total amount of $89,745.28 would satisfy the distribution order and that he intends to file a petition and give notice to all of the beneficiaries. The Petition for Distribution of Trust Funds, etc. is continued to March 5, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of a petition for final distribution is also set for March 5, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Notice Of Petition For Distribution Of Trust Funds In Payment Of Judgment And Child Support By Creditor Deanna Ireland
Off Calendar Dec-18-2017 Continued To Jan-29-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Daniel Conrad for and with Scott Hodges (Trustee).
Teresa Trucchi appeared by telephone for Deanna Ireland (Creditor).

Mr. Conrad stated that there is $13,000.00 in cash, that his Client pays for storage of the art work, the taxes and the CPA, that the art has no value, that they made many efforts to sell the art and that nobody is interested in buying it. Mr. Conrad also stated that there is a small lot in Idaho, that there is a fractional interest in real property in Washington, that in order to sell the Washington property they would have to hire an Attorney in Washington and do a partition action, that there is a lis pendens one of the properties by the US Government, that the Trust is illiquid and that there are no funds with which to pay. Ms. Trucchi stated that in 2010 the art work was valued at $3,000,000.00 and that the Trustee was instructed to sell it within two years. The Court suggested transfer of the real property and the art work for an agreed upon amount. Ms. Trucchi stated that she will have to speak with her Client. The Petition for Distribution of Trust Funds, etc. is continued to January 29, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Petition For Discretionary Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-15-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Conrad for and with (trustee) Scott Hodges. Samy Henein (via court call) for (petitioner) Deanna Ireland.

Mr. Conrad stated that the examiner's notes indicate that the trust is not under court supervision, that the assets consist of $75,000.00 in cash and $390,000.00 in real property, that under the trust Mr. Hodges has discretion regarding distribution, that not all of the relief asked for is applicable, cited 15305(c) & 15306.5, that Ian Ireland was incarcerated but is now out, that Mr. Hodges has been paying him an allowance, paying his rent and has paid for a contractor's license, that the allowance comes to $49,100.00 and the advance for the contractor's license was $1000.00, that $75,000.00 was distributed to Deanna Ireland in 2012, that $20,000.00 has been distributed to Sorin Ireland, that $10,000.00 has been distributed to Bailey Ireland, that the petition requests that Mr. Hodges be ordered to pay $89,000.00 to her, that it is more than what is available in the trust, that he is being sued, that Mr. Hodges asked for information regarding the minor's needs but did not receive a response and has been waiting to see what the court orders regarding Svea Ireland. Mr. Henein stated that judgment creditor can reach the trust, that the accounting shows that there is over $500,000.00 in assets, that a substantial distribution was made to Ian even though there is a lien, that there is enough cash to make the distribution, that previously there was $220,000.00 in cash on hand, that they addressed the probate examiner's notes, that a copy of the trust was provided as an attachment to the notice of lodgment filed on 2/25/16, that they provided the court with a copy of the accounting, that they have met all of the requirements and that there is no basis to refuse distribution. The Court stated that per Mr. Conrad there is only $75,000.00 in cash on hand. Mr. Henein stated that this is not consistent with the accounting they were provided, that as of 12/31/15 there was $91,214.00 in the Wells Fargo Savings and $13,276.00 in the Wells Fargo checking, that as of 2/28/13 there was $217,926.00 in the Wells Fargo Savings and $5000.00 in the Wells Fargo Checking and that in regards to the need for distribution they have a child support order. Mr. Conrad responded that February 2013 was a long time ago, that in 2015 per the accounting Deanna filed there was $102,000.00 but that was five months ago and that there is a total of $76,000.00 now in the two accounts. Mr. Conrad further stated that in the absence of information the facts are hard to weigh and that they do not object to making a distribution to Svea. The Court asked Mr. Henein what additional information could be filed and when could we get it. Mr. Henein stated that he would ask for a continuance so that Ms. Trucchi can talk to counsel regarding the market value of assets. Mr. Conrad stated that they would like an opportunity to respond to anything that is filed. The Court requested that the supplemental information from Ms. Trucchi be filed no later than May 25, 2016, that responses be filed no later than June 1, 2016 and that the matter will be submitted as of June 6, 2016.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Petition To Approve Stipulation And For Authorization To Pay Portion Of Child Support Judgement.
Off Calendar Aug-20-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Scott Hodges.

Mr. Siracusa explained that distribution was made to Ion prior to 2011 and that it was not done by his Client. Mr. Siracusa also stated that Mr. Ireland's daughter made a request for additional funds for school needs. The Court referred to the declaration of Deanna Ireland with regard to the funds being used for Svea's needs and approved the Petition to Approve Stipulation and for Authorization to Pay Portion of Child Support Judgment (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy C. Garcia.

Petition To Approve Stipulation And For Authorization To Pay Portion Of Child Support Judgement.
Off Calendar Aug-06-2012 Continued To Aug-20-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearance: Lawrence Siracusa for Scott Hodges (Petitioner).

Counsel addressed the issues with regard to disbursement from the trust to pay Ian's child support obligations, is it for Ian's benefit, what will the funds be used for, and the need and expenses to appoint a Guardian Ad Litem. The Petition to Approve Stipulation is continued to August 20, 2012 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 for further information or input from a Guardian Ad Litem.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy A. Whitehead.

Petition For Orders: (1) Settling The First Accounts; (2) Passing Upon The Acts Of The Trustees; (3) Accepting The Resignation Of A Trustee;
Off Calendar Feb-09-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Greene and Rebecca Renzas for Ann Hatch. Lawrence Siracusa for Scott Hodges (Proposed Trustee).

The Court stated that it had a chance to review the declaration and supporting documents, and that it had no questions. The Court also informed Mr. Greene and Ms. Renzas that it does not approve anticipated fees. The matter was taken under submission for review of the order. The amount of bond for the Successor Trustee will be provided to the Court by Ms. Renzas.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Orders: (1) Settling The First Accounts; (2) Passing Upon The Acts Of The Trustees; (3) Accepting The Resignation Of A Trustee;
Off Calendar Jan-12-2011 Continued To Feb-02-11 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Rebecca Renzas and Richard Greene for Ann Hatch (Petitioner). Jonathan Ferdon for Public Administrator/Public Guardian. Teresa Trucchi appeared by telephone for Deanna Ireland.

Counsel stated that the fees were split between the principal and the interest, that the Trustees waived there fees, that the Trustees knew that any excess funds would go to charity and that Ms. Hatch was waiving fees in Ian's Trust. Ms. Trucchi stated that she was fine with Mr. Hodges being appointed. The Court stated that the matter appeared to be in order, that it was inclined to waive notice to the Minor, that it was appropriate to allow Ms. Hatch to resign, and that it was appropriate to appoint Mr. Hodges as Successor Trustee in Ian's Trust. The Court also requested itemization of the fees and stated that it did not require any further consents. The matter was continued to February 2, 2011 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of itemization of the attorney's fees.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.
